plugin.name: MCP Steroid
plugin.description:
MCP Steroid brings the full power of the IntelliJ Platform to AI agents through the Model Context Protocol (MCP).

IntelliJ platform works for AI agents as great as for human developers.

  • 9 MCP Tools: Control IntelliJ IDEA programmatically — execute code, take screenshots, discover actions, debug, and more
  • 58 MCP Resources: Comprehensive guides covering LSP, IDE operations, debugger, tests, VCS, and more
  • Vision Capabilities: AI agents can see your IDE with screenshots and OCR
  • Deep Integration: Access PSI, inspections, refactorings, and full IntelliJ Platform API
  • Human Oversight: Review and approve code execution with configurable safety controls

Compatible with all IntelliJ Platform-based IDEs: IntelliJ IDEA, PyCharm, WebStorm, GoLand, CLion, Rider, and more.

Requirements: IntelliJ IDEA 2025.3 or newer (build 253 or later).

Visit mcp-steroid.jonnyzzz.com for documentation and examples.
plugin.change-notes:
What's New in v0.90.0

ClaudeCode debugs an application in Rider -- MCP Steroid

In this video, ClaudeCode drives Rider debugger workflows through MCP Steroid, including breakpoint setup, debugger control, and IDE-driven test execution.

DPAIA Arena: First Success Metrics

As of 2026-03-02, the first completed A/B comparison set shows:

  • Fix rate: `4/4` with MCP Steroid and `4/4` without MCP.
  • Average agent time: `412s` with MCP vs `526s` without MCP.
  • Net speedup: MCP was `22%` faster on average (`114s` gain per case).
  • Cost: MCP was about `10%` cheaper across our 4-test experiment set.
  • Strongest gains were on complex multi-file tasks (`21-27%` faster), while simple search-and-replace tasks were effectively neutral.

    • Prompt Platform Rework

  • Added a dedicated `prompts-api` module (`ArticlePart`, `IdeFilter`, `PromptsContext`) and reworked generation around article parts with computed filters.
  • Migrated prompt articles to the single-article model with line-2 filters and fence annotations, including multi-IDE filter support.
  • Replaced SkillCatalog YAML frontmatter with article properties and removed obsolete filter code paths.
  • Moved prompt quality gates into the prompts pipeline (`KtBlock` compilation and debugger prompt quality coverage).

    • Additional Validations

  • Added article quality contract tests and stricter prompt content checks.
  • Updated prompt examples to compilable fences and replaced deprecated IntelliJ 2025.3 API usages.
  • Included kotlinc version in `KtBlock` compilation cache keys to prevent stale cache reuse across compiler upgrades.
  • Hardened release/update validation by using actual GitHub release asset URLs for `updatePlugins.xml`, failing fast on missing `VERSION`/release URL values, and preventing non-plugin asset selection.

    • Rider and Debugger Workflow Maturity

  • Added Rider to IntelliJ debugger support coverage with dedicated Rider integration/debugger test flows.
  • Introduced RLVR-based Rider debugger prompt iteration: agents were trained in a virtual environment to test and refine prompts for reliable debugger support.
  • Added Rider IDE integration coverage for debugger workflows and introduced `RiderPlaygroundTest` for interactive debugging sessions.
  • Improved debugger prompts for event-driven suspension, idempotent breakpoints, and Rider/.NET expression handling.
  • Added run-test-at-caret prompt workflows and fixed action IDs/caret targeting in debugger demos.
  • Extracted shared debugger test helpers to reduce duplication between IntelliJ and Rider demo tests.

    • Build & Infrastructure

  • Extracted `intellij-downloader` and `kotlin-cli` modules and moved related tests into the new module boundaries.
  • Fixed IDE downloader platform resolution in containerized runs (correct archive per OS).
  • Improved integration-run stability via warm snapshot bootstrap hardening and test-suite parallelism controls.
  • Fixed ScriptExecutor timeout test behavior and excluded Docker CLI tests from the default fast suite.

    • Full release notes: mcp-steroid.jonnyzzz.com/releases/0.90.0/
